title: Method and apparatus of slice-level signaling for bi-directional optical flow and decoder side motion vector refinement
abstract: Embodiments described herein disclose a method for encoding a video sequence and for decoding an encoded video sequence where slice-level information related to one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction is provided in case the slice is a B-slice by the encoder in the encoded video sequence and this information is used by the decoder to perform the inter prediction using the motion refinement video coding tools obtained from the information. Further, an encoder and a decoder implemented embodiments of such a method a disclosed.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for encoding a video sequence using one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction, wherein the method comprises: - determining whether a type of a slice of the video sequence allows bi-directional interprediction, - obtaining slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction only if the slice type allows bi-directional inter-prediction; - obtaining block-level level or coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; - performing bi-directional inter-prediction with motion refinement according to the slice-level and the block-level or the coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; encoding the video by encoding at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; and - adding the slice level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction a bitstream. 2. A method for decoding an encoded video sequence using one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction, wherein the method comprises: - obtaining slice level information related to a type of a slice of the video sequence; - determining whether the type of the slice of the encoded video sequence allows bidirectional inter-prediction, - obtaining slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction only if the slice type allows bi-directional inter-prediction; - obtaining block-level or coding unit level information related to motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; - performing bi-directional inter-prediction of at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction according to the slice- level and block-level or coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; and decoding the at least one block of the video sequence by decoding the at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction is signaled only for B- slices. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction is inferred to be equal to values that disable said one or more motion refinement video coding tools when it is not signaled for B-slices. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the method comprises: - obtaining sequence parameter set (SPS)-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; and - obtaining slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction only if the SPS-level information allows signaling slice-level information for the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the one or more motion refinement video coding tool for bi-directional prediction comprise or is BDOF. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the one or more motion refinement video coding tool for bi-directional prediction comprise or is DMVR. 8. The method according to any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the slice-level information is included in a bitstream. 9. The method according to claim 8, wherein the slice-level information is included in a header of the bitstream. 10. The method according to claim 8 or 9, wherein the bitstream is or compirses the encoded video sequence. 11. The method according to any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the slice-level information indicates the one or more motion refinement tools to be used in the prediction. 12. The method according to any of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the slice-level information comprises a flag indicating the one or more motion refinement tools to be used in the prediction. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the size of the flag is 1 bit. 14. The method according to any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction comprise or are at least one of BDOF and DMVR. 15. An encoder comprising processing circuitry configured for performing the method according to any of claims 1 and 3 to 14. 16. A decoder comprising processing circuitry configured for performing the method according to any of claims 2 to 14. 17. An encoder, comprising: one or more processors; and a non-transitory storage medium coupled to the processors and storing programming for execution by the processors, wherein the programming, when executed by the processors, configures the encoder to carry out the method according to any one of claims 1 and 3 to 14. 18. A decoder, comprising: one or more processors; and a non-transitory storage medium coupled to the processors and storing programming for execution by the processors, wherein the programming, when executed by the processors, configures the decoder to carry out the method according to any of claims 2 to 14. 19. A program product comprising a program code for performing the method according to any of claims 1 to 14. 20. A non-transitory medium carrying a program code which, when executed by a device, causes the device to perform the method of any of claims 1 to 14. 21. An encoder for encoding a video sequence using one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction, the encoder comprising: A prediction unit; An encoding unit; Wherein the prediction unit is adapted to: - determine whether a type of a slice of the video sequence allows bi-directional interprediction, - obtain slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction only if the slice type allows bi-directional inter-prediction; 

 - obtain block-level level or coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; - perform bi-directional inter-prediction with motion refinement according to the slice-level and the block-level or the coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; Wherein the encoding unit is adapted to: - encode the video by encoding at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; and - add the slice level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction a bitstream. 22. A decoder for decoding an encoded video sequence using one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction, the decoder comprising: A receiving unit; A decoding unit; Wherein the receiving unit is adapted to: - receive the encoded video sequence; - obtain slice level information related to a type of a slice of the video sequence; - determine whether the type of the slice of the encoded video sequence allows bi-directional inter-prediction, - obtain slice-level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction only if the slice type allows bi-directional inter-prediction; - obtain block-level or coding unit level information related to motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; Wherein the decoding unit is adapted to: - perform bi-directional inter-prediction of at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction according to the slice-level and block-level or coding unit level information related to the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction; and - decode the at least one block of the video sequence by decoding the at least one block predicted using the one or more motion refinement video coding tools for bi-directional prediction.
